Worker dies after going overboard from Princess cruise ship that left Fort Lauderdale
items.summary: A crew member has died after going overboard from a Princess cruise ship that left Fort Lauderdale, Florida, the company said Monday.

items.title: How Lindsey Graham’s death quickly changes a busy Senate: From the Politics Desk
items.summary: Welcome to From the Politics Desk, a daily newsletter that brings you the NBC News Politics team’s latest reporting and analysis from the White House, Capitol Hill and the campaign trail.

items.title: U.S. announces more strikes on Iran over Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: President Trump said that the United States will blockade Iran in the Strait of Hormuz and charge ships 20% for safe passage. Central Command announced that the U.S. has commenced strikes on Iran for the third straight night. NBC News' Courtney Kube reports.

items.title: Dani Olmo y la elección sobre Croacia: "Mi sueño siempre fue España", | Rumbo a la Gran Final
items.summary: El cerebro silencioso de la "Roja" recordó sus inicios en Croacia, el cariño que le tiene al país donde creció como profesional y el momento de elegir selección. Olmo aparece entre líneas, acelera y es clave sin buscar los reflectores ni el protagonismo.

items.title: 2 teens suspected in shooting spree that killed 5 people from same family in Illinois
items.summary: Police and prosecutors declined to name the 16- and 15-year-old suspects or even reveal their genders a day after they were captured in East St. Louis.

items.title: Valdano: las Malvinas, Maradona y la Semifinal que estremece a Argentina | Rumbo a la Gran Final
items.summary: El Campeón del Mundo recordó al Diego Maradona épico del 86 y la herida reciente por las Malvinas en aquel duelo histórico . Además, apuntó a la nueva generación que vivirá por primera vez esta rivalidad en la Semifinal de la Copa del Mundo.

items.title: S.C. Gov McMaster appoints Darline Graham Nordone to fill Sen. Lindsey Graham's seat
items.summary: South Carolina Governor Henry McMaster appoints Darline Graham Nordone, Sen. Lindsey Graham's younger sister, to serve the remainder of the late Republican senator's term, which ends early January 2027.

items.title: Trump's HHS abandons threat to withhold Medicare and Medicaid funding over trans care
items.summary: NPR has learned that the Department of Health and Human Services will not be finalizing its most aggressive attempt to end gender-affirming care for youth nationally.

items.title: South Carolina's governor names Lindsey Graham's sister to serve out his term
items.summary: South Carolina's governor says he is appointing Darline Graham to finish the term of her late brother. Top Republicans, including President Trump, have coalesced around the pick.

items.title: Argentina por la historia: las 5 veces que se ha enfrentado a Inglaterra en los Mundiales
items.summary: Una de las rivalidades más apasionantes del fútbol mundial vuelve a arder esta Copa con la Semifinal; tres derrotas y dos victorias pesan en la memoria de la "Albiceleste", que llega con todo el fuego de la historia para escribir una nueva página épica.

items.title: On foreign policy, Graham's influence may have been felt the most
items.summary: Over the course of his three decades in Congress, Lindsey Graham was a major influence on Capitol Hill and throughout the world as one of the most vocal advocates for U.S. military might.

items.title: Batistuta: "La rivalidad sirve para alimentar el deseo de ganar" | Rumbo a la Gran Final
items.summary: El histórico goleador de la Selección Argentina recordó cómo vivió los duelos ante Inglaterra en los Mundiales, el entorno que rodea esta rivalidad y lo que comparte con Lionel Scaloni de cara a la Semifinal de la Copa del Mundo de este miércoles.

items.title: Minnesota prosecutors obtain long withheld evidence in protest shooting deaths
items.summary: Minnesota prosecutors have obtained key evidence in their investigations into the fatal shootings of Renee Good and Alex Pretti

items.title: Los goles de Messi y Mbappé en el Mundial como nunca antes los habías visto
items.summary: La cámara de los árbitros en la Copa del Mundo 2026 nos regaló una perspectiva muy distinta de los golazos que se marcaron en esta edición… Es IMPERDIBLE.

items.title: Judge says Trump admin tried to ‘manipulate the judicial process’ with its IRS settlement
items.summary: A federal judge in Florida has found President Donald Trump’s lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service was brought in “bad faith” for an “improper purpose.”

items.title: Hard drives, car obtained in investigation of shootings of Renee Good, Alex Pretti
items.summary: Minnesota prosecutors obtain hard drives, car in investigation into fatal shootings of Renee Good and Alex Pretti

items.title: South Carolina governor appoints Lindsey Graham’s sister to finish his Senate term
items.summary: The announcement from Gov. Henry McMaster came after President Donald Trump recommended Graham’s sister, Darline Graham Nordone, for the temporary position.

items.title: The U.S. strikes Iran after Trump announces a renewed blockade and tolls in Hormuz
items.summary: The U.S. and Iran are fighting for control of the Strait of Hormuz, threatening a return to all-out war after agreeing to a ceasefire last month.

items.title: Man fatally shot by ICE officer in Maine was not the target of arrest warrant, senator’s spokesman says
items.summary: The man killed in a shooting involving ICE officers in Biddeford, Maine, was not the intended target of an arrest warrant, DHS Secretary Markwayne Mullin told Sen.

items.title: 12 states file lawsuit to block $110 billion Paramount-Warner Bros. merger
items.summary: Twelve state attorneys general filed a lawsuit challenging Paramount Skydance’s $110 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery. NBC News' Allie Canal breaks down how the lawsuit says consumers could be impacted and what it could mean for Paramount.

items.title: States sue to stop Paramount-Warner Bros blockbuster merger
items.summary: California is among the states suing to block Paramount from buying Warner Bros. Discovery in a Hollywood mega-merger that would unite some of the nation's largest movie studios and TV newsrooms.

items.title: Person reportedly shot and killed in incident involving ICE agent in Maine
items.summary: A person has been reportedly shot and killed in an incident involving an ICE agent in Biddeford, Maine. NBC News' Julia Ainsley has early details on the incident and how local and federal authorities are responding.

items.title: US citizen is found guilty of helping export tech to Iran in violation of sanctions
items.summary: A Massachusetts man has been found guilty of conspiring to unlawfully export electronic components to Iran, violating U.S. sanctions

items.title: Oil surges more than 9% after Trump reimposes Iran blockade
items.summary: Trump said the U.S. “will be reimbursed” for helping ships through the Strait of Hormuz. Crude oil hit its highest price in about a month.

items.title: Jay-Z apologizes after Yankee Stadium show starts hours late, says there was a safety issue
items.summary: Sunday night’s show wrapped Jay-Z’s three-night hometown event at the stadium to celebrate the 30th anniversary of his debut album, “Reasonable Doubt.”

items.title: Who could replace Lindsey Graham? South Carolina's next steps after senator's death
items.summary: Sen. Lindsey Graham's death has sparked a political scramble in South Carolina

items.title: Crash of an aircraft helping to fight a Colorado wildfire leaves the pilot dead
items.summary: Authorities say an aircraft helping to fight a Colorado wildfire has crashed into a reservoir, killing the pilot

items.title: The U.S.-Iran ceasefire grows more distant. And, Congress faces a consequential week
items.summary: The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire over the weekend amid tensions over the Strait of Hormuz. And, Congress returns from recess facing tight deadlines.

items.title: Massive AI buildout poses inflation threat as consumers pay more for electricity
items.summary: American consumers — and the Federal Reserve — are being hit with another high-cost headache

items.title: Philadelphia area digs out from damage left by a severe series of microburst storms
items.summary: Residents of Philadelphia and some surrounding counties are cleaning up and assessing damage after a series of severe storms called microbursts swept through with winds up to 60 or 70 miles per hour

items.title: Pythons' extreme biology may hold clues for treating human disease
items.summary: These snakes can go for months without eating, grow and shrink the size of their hearts and jump start their metabolism on a dime.

items.title: A guard punched him on camera. It was still nearly impossible for him to sue
items.summary: Prisoners are allowed to file complaints about mistreatment — but doing so often comes with retaliation. That's according to an investigation by NPR and The Marshall Project.

items.title: Eat your ice cream for a long, healthy life? This doctor says so
items.summary: Dr. Zeke Emanuel, a renowned U.S. health policy physician, takes on the wellness industry with a back-to-basics approach to optimizing good health.

items.title: He was having a mental health crisis. Memphis task force agents came and shot him
items.summary: Jonah Neal, 25, was struck by a Homeland Security Investigations agent in May. There have been at least four deadly shootings related to the task force.

items.title: Jordan says it shot down four missiles launched by Iran
items.summary: Jordan says it shot down four missiles launched by Iran

items.title: Man who killed Dartmouth professors at 17 seeks reduced prison sentence
items.summary: A Vermont man who was 17 when he and a friend killed a pair of married Dartmouth College professors 25 years ago wants his life sentence reduced to a minimum of 30 to 40 years

items.title: Senate returns to Washington after Sen. Lindsey Graham's death with uncertain agenda
items.summary: Senate Republicans will return to Washington with an uncertain agenda after the sudden death of prominent Republican Lindsey Graham, a committee chairman and key player who served as a crucial ally with President Donald Trump

items.title: Ramiro Córdoba: “Francia es muy favorita sobre España” | El Pelotazo
items.summary: El seleccionado francés es el equipo que mejor fútbol ha jugado y ser muy fuerte en todas sus líneas, además que cualquiera de sus jugadores pueden marcar la diferencia.

items.title: Bahrain sounds its missile alert sirens for a second time as Iran retaliates over US airstrikes
items.summary: Bahrain sounds its missile alert sirens for a second time as Iran retaliates over US airstrikes

items.title: Roque Santa Cruz: “Paraguay dio un paso importante” | Rumbo a la gran final
items.summary: El seleccionado paraguayo sorprendió al mundo al eliminar a Alemania y plantarle cara a Francia, demostrando un gran crecimiento con jugadores que militan en las mejores ligas

items.title: Dely Valdés: “Faltó el gol de Panamá” | Rumbo a la gran final
items.summary: El experto analizó el desempeño del seleccionado canalero y aunque reconoció un crecimiento, también sabe que necesitan apoyar el proyecto y no truncarlo.

items.title: Antonio Valencia: “Es hora de que Ecuador gane algo” | Rumbo a la gran final
items.summary: Nuestro experto resaltó la actuación frente a Alemania, pero dejó mucho que desear ante Cabo Verde y ante México, pero confía en que para el próximo mundial serán un equipo más fuerte.

items.title: Dunga: “Brasil debe volver a ser Brasil” | Rumbo a la Gran Final
items.summary: El exfutbolista reconoce que la Canarinha ha perdido la esencia que tantos títulos les dio y su eliminación temprana causó mucha tristeza.

items.title: Innovation, data fixes fuel Native American graduation gains at federally funded schools
items.summary: The U.S. agency that oversees dozens of schools serving Native Americans is reporting more on-time high school graduations than ever

items.title: Georgia teen charged in Apalachee High School shooting to appear in court for plea and sentencing
items.summary: A judge has set a plea and sentencing hearing for a teenager accused of killing four people in a 2024 shooting at Apalachee High School in Georgia

items.title: Democrat announces whistleblower allegations of construction problems at Kennedy Center
items.summary: A Democratic senator says whistleblowers have alleged numerous problems with rushed reconstruction of the Kennedy Center

items.title: Mexican builder fatally shot by an ICE officer is mourned after making a life in the US
items.summary: The builder got up every morning long before dawn, left home to pick up his construction crew and then headed out to work on yet another house somewhere across the sprawl of Houston

items.title: Judge tosses remnants of Proud Boys seditious conspiracy case after Trump's broad clemency
items.summary: A federal judge has dismissed the remnants of the government’s landmark case against far-right Proud Boys members who were convicted of seditious conspiracy

items.title: New York Times reporters are subpoenaed after Air Force One reporting, newspaper says
items.summary: The New York Times reports the Trump administration has subpoenaed several of its journalists after their report over security concerns with the new Air Force One

items.title: A July 4 boat trip, an 18-year-old's death and a family's search for answers in the Deep South
items.summary: A week ago, 18-year-old Nolan Xavier Wells took a boat trip with friends to celebrate the Fourth of July on an island off Mississippi’s Gulf Coast

items.title: Trump threatens Iran after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral saw open calls for his killing
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump has threatened Iran after the funeral of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei saw open calls for his killing

items.title: Trump administration rolls back a key protection for imperiled wildlife
items.summary: The Trump administration has finalized a rule that changes how agencies enforce the Endangered Species Act

items.title: Emergency crews rescue about 20 after campground building collapses in flooding
items.summary: Emergency crews in Missouri rescued about 20 people on Friday after a building collapsed at a campground

items.title: Both engines flamed out before small jet crashed in June on highway, NTSB report says
items.summary: Both engines flamed out on a small business jet that crashed on a Texas highway in June, killing one person and injuring six, preventing the pilots from being able to reach the Laredo airport

items.title: County seeks help to care for 16 siblings from squalid home, prosecute their family
items.summary: The discovery of 16 siblings who authorities say were held at a home for years in squalid conditions is straining their rural Ohio county’s resources as it works to prosecute their parents and two grandparents and address the needs of so many children ...

items.title: Lawyers for man charged with killing Charlie Kirk question reliability of evidence
items.summary: Lawyers for the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk plan to call a final witness on Friday as they try to raise doubts about the prosecution’s case

items.title: As the country turns 250, retired judges hit the road to defend judicial independence
items.summary: A group of retired federal and state judges has been barnstorming through Ohio and Pennsylvania on the nation's 250th anniversary to defend judicial independence and bolster the rule of law

items.title: New Year's Eve ball drops in Times Square for America's 250th
items.summary: The New Year's Eve ball in Times Square has dropped for America's 250th anniversary, making this the first time the ball has dropped outside of a New Year's celebration.

items.title: GOP prepared to block vote to replace Feinstein on Senate Judiciary
items.summary: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer said on Tuesday that he hopes to replace Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland and aims to set up a floor vote on the issue this afternoon, which Republicans are expected to block.

items.title: Supreme Court seems sympathetic to postal worker who didn't work Sundays in dispute over religious accommodations
items.summary: The Supreme Court seemed to side with a former mail carrier, an evangelical Christian, who says the US Postal Service failed to accommodate his request to not work on Sundays.

items.title: Anti-abortion doctors urge Supreme Court to keep mifepristone restrictions in place
items.summary: A group of doctors opposed to abortion asked the Supreme Court Tuesday to restrict access to a key medication abortion drug while other legal challenges play out, as Wednesday night's deadline for the court to act approaches.

items.title: McCarthy makes plea for Republicans to back debt ceiling plan
items.summary: Speaker Kevin McCarthy made a plea to House Republicans during a closed-door meeting Tuesday morning to back his debt ceiling plan, telling them that although it doesn't have to include everything they want, it will help get him to the negotiating table with President Joe Biden.

items.title: Fulton County, Georgia, jail leadership resigns after inmate's death and accusations of unsanitary conditions
items.summary: Three officials at the Fulton County, Georgia, jail have stepped down amid an investigation into the death of an inmate whose family said was housed in a filthy, bug-infested cell that "was not fit for a diseased animal."

items.title: China's economy is off to a solid start, rising 4.5% in Q1 2023
items.summary: China's economy is off to a solid start in 2023 following its emergence from three years of strict pandemic restrictions.

items.title: He was free for 2 years. Now Crosley Green is back in prison for a crime he says he didn't commit
items.summary: A Florida man who served three decades behind bars for a murder he says he didn't commit returned to prison Monday after spending the past two years building a life outside prison walls.

items.title: Family of victim and survivors of Indianapolis FedEx mass shooting file lawsuit against gun magazine manufacturer and distributors
items.summary: The family of a victim and several survivors of a mass shooting at a FedEx facility in Indianapolis filed a lawsuit against companies involved in the manufacturing, marketing and sale of the high capacity magazine used by the gunman who killed 8 people and injured several others in 2021.

items.title: McDonald's is upgrading its burgers
items.summary: McDonald's, which has been focusing on upgrading its core items to boost sales, is rolling out a series of changes designed to improve its signature burgers.

items.title: Google-parent stock drops on fears it could lose search market share to AI-powered rivals
items.summary: Shares of Google-parent Alphabet fell more than 3% in early trading Monday after a report sparked concerns that its core search engine could lose market share to AI-powered rivals, including Microsoft's Bing.

items.title: Tennessee Air National Guardsman applied to be a hitman online, the FBI says. It was a spoof website and now he's facing charges
items.summary: A Tennessee Air National Guardsman is facing charges after applying to be a hitman on a spoof "rent-a-hitman" website, according to the Department of Justice.

items.title: Even when wives make as much as husbands, they still do more at home
items.summary: • Four out of the five US metro areas with the lowest unemployment are in Florida. Here's why • Opinion: The overlooked problem with raising the retirement age for Social Security
